frameworks.name: lazy vs rich training dynamics
frameworks.one_line: neural network transitions from kernel-like lazy regime to feature-learning rich regime

frameworks.name: neural tangent kernel
frameworks.one_line: initial NTK fits a kernel regression solution that fails to generalize

frameworks.name: feature learning theory
frameworks.one_line: late-time feature learning identifies a generalizing solution after train loss is low

frameworks.name: grokking phenomenon
frameworks.one_line: delayed generalization where test loss drops long after train loss

core_question: Can grokking be explained as a transition from lazy to rich training dynamics in a neural network?
key_assumptions: two-layer network on polynomial regression suffices to illustrate the mechanism
sufficient statistics for test loss can be identified and tracked
output-scaling parameters control the rate of feature learning
manipulations: output-scale parameters controlling feature learning rate
alignment between initial NTK top eigenvectors and target labels
dataset size relative to generalization threshold
key_findings: grokking arises without regularization in this simple setting
network first fits a kernel regression solution with initial features
late-time feature learning later yields a generalizing solution
key determinants are feature-learning rate and initial-feature/target alignment
interpretations: grokking reflects a lazy-to-rich training transition
delayed generalization requires NTK-label misalignment plus an intermediate dataset-size regime
